subservice: Building Permit
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Building Permit
Department: BUILDING REGULATORY SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit all required documents for Building Permit application to the Building Regulatory Services Office
Step 2: Agency checks document completeness (20 minutes)
Step 3: Agency routes documents through Building Permit and Evaluation Division for initial review; records transaction in automated system; sends applicant a text with transaction number (30 minutes)
Step 4: Agency reviews and evaluates documents and plans across all technical disciplines (Architectural, Civil/Structural, Sanitary, Mechanical, Electrical, Electronic) and calculates fees (3 days)
Step 5: Agency prints order of payment and conducts technical discipline assessment (3 days)
Step 6: Building Official approves order of payment (3 days)
Step 7: Client receives order of payment and remits fees to the City Treasury Office
Step 8: Treasury issues Official Receipt (1 day)
Step 9: Client submits receipt, order of payment, and approved clearances (including Fire Safety and DOLE documentation)
Step 10: Agency organizes permit documents and assigns building permit number (1 day)
Step 11: Building Official approves building permit (1 day)
Step 12: Agency scans approved documents and plans for archival

subservice: Permit to Operate and Annual Inspection
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Permit to Operate and Annual Inspection
Department: BUILDING REGULATORY SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Prepare required documents and receive inspection schedule notice from agency (15 minutes)
Step 2: Submit to inspector for scheduling; agency conducts annual inspection following standard procedures (60 minutes)
Step 3: Visit Building Official's office to arrange payment; agency issues payment order and notes compliance requirements (15 minutes)
Step 4: Collect Annual Inspection Certificate / Permit to Operate; agency prints, signs, and issues certificate (5 minutes)

subservice: Certificate of Occupancy
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of Occupancy
Department: BUILDING REGULATORY SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit all required documentation to the Building Regulatory Services Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews materials (1 day)
Step 3: Building Standards Office inspects the establishment; inspector prepares inspection report and payment order (1 day)
Step 4: Client receives payment order based on prescribed rates under Presidential Decree No. 1096
Step 5: Agency prepares the occupancy certificate; Building Official and designated engineers sign the certificate (1 day)
Step 6: Client collects the Certificate of Occupancy and Permit to Operate (if applicable) (1 day)

subservice: Certificate of Final Electrical Inspection
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of Final Electrical Inspection
Department: BUILDING REGULATORY SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit the MERALCO yellow card and required documentation to BRSO; staff reviews all documents (2 minutes)
Step 2: Building Official or authorized engineer conducts on-site facility inspection (3 days)
Step 3: BRSO staff issues payment order; applicant pays PHP 150.00 per certificate (1 minute)
Step 4: Applicant collects the Certificate of Final Electrical Inspection (1 minute)

subservice: Serbisyong One Stop Shop
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Serbisyong One Stop Shop
Department: BUILDING REGULATORY SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit all required documents, plans, and clearance forms to the Building Regulatory Services Office
Step 2: Agency checks completeness; documents entered into Business Permit Automated System; transaction number sent via text (20 minutes)
Step 3: CPDO evaluates locational clearance (30 minutes); Real Property Tax assessed (30 minutes); Bureau of Fire conducts safety evaluation (3 days); specialists review all plans
Step 4: Order of Payment generated and approved by Building Official
Step 5: Client pays required fees at treasury; official receipt issued (1 day)
Step 6: Documents organized and building permit number assigned (5 minutes); Building Official provides final approval
Step 7: Approved documents scanned for archiving; client retrieves approved Building Permit

subservice: Barangay Financial Reports
department: CITY ACCOUNTING AND INTERNAL CONTROL OFFICE
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Barangay Financial Reports
Department: CITY ACCOUNTING AND INTERNAL CONTROL OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit financial reports within the first five (5) days of the following month
Step 2: Administrative Officer V and Supervising Administrative Officer review reports for completeness (15 minutes)
Step 3: City Accountant signs off and submits documents to Commission on Audits (COA) (5 minutes)

subservice: Certificate of Contribution (GSIS, Philhealth, Pag-Ibig)
department: CITY ACCOUNTING AND INTERNAL CONTROL OFFICE
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of Contribution (GSIS, Philhealth, Pag-Ibig)
Department: CITY ACCOUNTING AND INTERNAL CONTROL OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request and required documents to the City Accounting and Internal Control Office
Step 2: Agency receives, reviews, and processes the request
Step 3: Agency issues the Certificate of Contribution for GSIS, Philhealth, and/or Pag-Ibig as requested

subservice: Permit for Posting Banners, Tarpaulins, Streamer on public places
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Permit for Posting Banners, Tarpaulins, Streamer on public places
Department: CITY ADMINISTRATION OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request letter containing: materials to be used (fabric/tarpaulin), design, dimensions, quantity, and location details
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews the request letter; discusses with client if needed and approves the permit
Step 3: Agency issues an Order of Payment to the client
Step 4: Client pays at City Treasury Office (PHP 400 per tarpaulin piece)
Step 5: Treasury provides official receipt
Step 6: Client receives the permit from Administrative Services; receipt details recorded and permit officially issued (11 minutes total)

subservice: Certificate of Kinship
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of Kinship
Department: CITY ADMINISTRATION OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to the City Administration Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency processes and prepares the Certificate of Kinship
Step 4: Client receives the Certificate of Kinship

subservice: Certificate of Good Moral Character for applying for a job abroad
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of Good Moral Character for applying for a job abroad
Department: CITY ADMINISTRATION OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to the City Administration Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency processes and prepares the Certificate of Good Moral Character
Step 4: Client receives the Certificate of Good Moral Character for job abroad application

subservice: Certificate of Residency for applying for a pension abroad
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of Residency for applying for a pension abroad
Department: CITY ADMINISTRATION OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to the City Administration Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency processes and prepares the Certificate of Residency
Step 4: Client receives the Certificate of Residency for pension abroad application

subservice: Issuance of New Tax Declaration for General Purposes
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Issuance of New Tax Declaration for General Purposes
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application and all required documents to the City Assessment Office
Step 2: Agency receives and examines documentation; if complete, issues Order of Payment and processes; if incomplete, returns documents
Step 3: Agency conducts property inspection, review, and assessment (2 days)
Step 4: Client pays at City Treasurer's Office (PHP 30.00 per request; 5 minutes)
Step 5: Agency issues the New Tax Declaration to the client (2 minutes)

subservice: Updating and Issuance of Tax Declaration from Transferred Properties
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Updating and Issuance of Tax Declaration from Transferred Properties
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit photocopies and present original documents to the City Assessment Office
Step 2: Agency receives and examines documentation; if complete, provides Order of Payment; if incomplete, returns documents (15 minutes)
Step 3: Agency conducts inspection, review, and assessment of the property (2 days)
Step 4: Client pays at the City Treasurer's Office (PHP 30.00 per request; 5 minutes)
Step 5: Agency provides copy of new Tax Declaration to client (2 minutes)

subservice: Issuance of Certified True Copy of Tax Declaration / Certificate of Aggregate Land Holdings
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Issuance of Certified True Copy of Tax Declaration / Certificate of Aggregate Land Holdings
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request and required documentation to the City Assessment Office; agency receives and reviews, issues Order of Payment (5 minutes)
Step 2: Agency processes request and applies official seals/annotations (8 minutes)
Step 3: Client pays at City Treasury Office (PHP 30.00; 2 minutes)
Step 4: Client collects certified copies from agency (3 minutes)

subservice: Certificate of No Improvement
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Certificate of No Improvement
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request and required documents to the City Assessment Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency processes the request and prepares the Certificate of No Improvement
Step 4: Client pays applicable fee at City Treasury Office
Step 5: Client receives the Certificate of No Improvement

subservice: Issuance of Barangay Jurisdiction
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Issuance of Barangay Jurisdiction
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request and required documents to the City Assessment Office
Step 2: Agency receives and examines documents
Step 3: Agency processes and issues the Barangay Jurisdiction document
Step 4: Client receives the Barangay Jurisdiction certificate

subservice: Senior Citizens ID
department: OFFICE FOR THE SENIOR CITIZENS AFFAIRS
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Senior Citizens ID
Department: OFFICE FOR THE SENIOR CITIZENS AFFAIRS
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to OSCA; staff receives and examines requirements (2 minutes)
Step 2: Officer conducts examination and interviews the applicant (5 minutes)
Step 3: Officer approves processed documentation (1 minute)
Step 4: Staff distributes the Senior Citizens ID card and grocery/medicine booklet; transaction recorded in logbook (2 minutes)

subservice: Issuance of PWD ID, Medicine, and Purchase Booklet
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Issuance of PWD ID, Medicine, and Purchase Booklet
Department: PERSONS WITH DISABILITY AFFAIRS OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit completed application form along with all required documentation to PDAO
Step 2: Staff receives and examines submitted documents (5 minutes)
Step 3: Administrative Officer II/III conducts assessment and client interview (5 minutes)
Step 4: Administrative Officer II/III approves documents
Step 5: Staff processes and delivers PWD ID, Purchase Booklet, and Medicine Booklet to client (5 minutes)

subservice: Processing and Issuance of Solo Parent ID
text:
Service: Certificates, Permits, and IDs
Sub-service: Processing and Issuance of Solo Parent ID
Department: CITY SOCIAL SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit all required documents to the City Social Services Department
Step 2: Staff examines documents and conducts pre-assessment to determine eligibility (10 minutes)
Step 3: Social worker conducts home visitation to verify eligibility (1 hour)
Step 4: Social worker processes the Solo Parent ID (10 minutes)
Step 5: Staff notifies the applicant via text or phone call; applicant receives Solo Parent ID within 20 days (5 minutes)

subservice: Application for New Business Permit (Onsite and Online)
department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
text:
Service: Business and Trade
Sub-service: Application for New Business Permit (Onsite and Online)
Department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
Steps:
ONSITE — Step 1 (File): Submit completed application form and all required documents to BPTFO
ONSITE — Step 2: Agency receives and verifies submitted form and documents; checks applicant's previous records (5 minutes)
ONSITE — Step 3: Agency routes application to regulatory offices: CPDO, BRSO, CENRO, CHO, Tourism, BFP, POSO, Assessor
ONSITE — Step 4: Agency determines compliance status; encodes compliant applications and forwards to treasury
ONSITE — Step 5 (Pay): Proceed to City Treasury counter at B.O.S.S. Area and settle all fees per Calamba City Tax Code of 2006
ONSITE — Step 6 (Claim): Collect copies of Business Permit, Barangay Clearance, Sanitary Permit, Business Plate, and stickers from releasing personnel
ONLINE — Step 1: Register and log in at https://online.calambacity.gov.ph
ONLINE — Step 2: Complete online application form and upload required documents
ONLINE — Step 3: Await approval notification via email
ONLINE — Step 4 (Pay): Settle fees through GCash, Maya, or Landbank payment gateways
ONLINE — Step 5 (Claim): Receive electronic business permit via email; obtain physical copies from BPTFO Releasing Section

subservice: Application for Business Permit Renewal (On-Site by Schedule / Walk-In)
department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
text:
Service: Business and Trade
Sub-service: Application for Business Permit Renewal (On-Site by Schedule / Walk-In)
Department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1 (File): Submit completed application form and all requirements to BPTFO for evaluation and verification
Step 2: BPTFO staff reviews submitted documents (5 minutes)
Step 3: If business is on negative list, route to regulatory offices — CPDO (2 min), BRSO (2 min), CENRO (2 min), CHO (2 min), Tourism (2 min), BFP (2 min), POSO (2 min), Assessor (2 min)
Step 4: Agency encodes compliant application in system (2 minutes)
Step 5: City Treasury approves application and generates Statement of Account (2 minutes)
Step 6 (Pay): Present at City Treasury counter in B.O.S.S. Area and remit fees (5 minutes)
Step 7 (Claim): Collect Business Permit, Barangay Clearance, Sanitary Permit, stickers from releasing personnel (13 minutes)

subservice: Amendment of Business Registration
department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
text:
Service: Business and Trade
Sub-service: Amendment of Business Registration
Department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit completed application and all required documents to BPTFO
Step 2: Agency receives and verifies documents; updates business records in system
Step 3: Agency processes the amendment and issues updated Business Permit
Step 4: Client pays applicable fees at City Treasury Office
Step 5: Client collects amended Business Registration documents

subservice: Motorized Tricycle Operators Permit
department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
text:
Service: Business and Trade
Sub-service: Motorized Tricycle Operators Permit
Department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1 (File Documents): Submit required documentation to the agency; agency accepts and verifies documents and updates operator records in system; agency prints Unified Tricycle Application Form (UTAF) for client signature and unit stencil (22 minutes)
Step 2 (Process & Approval): Agency retrieves requirements and approves application in system for Treasury Office billing (5 minutes)
Step 3 (Payment): Client pays PHP 1,520.00 at Treasurer's Office located in BPTFO; agency receives payment and provides official receipt (5 minutes)
Step 4 (Claim Permit): Client receives approved tricycle franchise document; agency issues franchise and records in release logbook (13 minutes)

subservice: Certificate of Termination of Franchise Use
department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
text:
Service: Business and Trade
Sub-service: Certificate of Termination of Franchise Use
Department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to the Business Permits and Tricycle Franchising Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency processes and issues the Certificate of Termination of Franchise Use
Step 4: Client receives the certificate

subservice: Retirement of Business Registration
department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
text:
Service: Business and Trade
Sub-service: Retirement of Business Registration
Department: BUSINESS PERMITS AND TRICYCLE FRANCHISING OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit completed application and required documents for business retirement/closure to BPTFO
Step 2: Agency receives and verifies documents; checks business records in system
Step 3: Agency processes the retirement and updates business registration status
Step 4: Agency issues Certificate of Retirement of Business Registration
Step 5: Client collects the retirement certificate

subservice: Providing Various Knowledge and Skills for Disaster Preparedness
department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
text:
Service: Disaster and Weather
Sub-service: Providing Various Knowledge and Skills for Disaster Preparedness
Department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request letter (Sulat Kahilingan) to the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Division
Step 2: Agency receives and examines the request letter
Step 3: Agency staff interviews the client to determine training needs
Step 4: Agency conducts the seminar, training, or webinar (2-3 days for Standard First Aid/Basic Life Support; 30 minutes to 3 hours for other trainings)

subservice: Emergency Response to Accidents and Disasters
department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
text:
Service: Disaster and Weather
Sub-service: Emergency Response to Accidents and Disasters
Department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
Steps:
Step 1: Citizen calls or texts to report accident details (2-minute response time by Dispatcher/LDRRMD Staff)
Step 2: Agency alerts the nearest Barangay Quick Response Team of the incident location (5 minutes)
Step 3: Medical personnel and responders travel to accident location and provide on-site assistance (10-minute arrival target)

subservice: Request for the Removal of Trees or Branches Posing a Safety Hazard
department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
text:
Service: Disaster and Weather
Sub-service: Request for the Removal of Trees or Branches Posing a Safety Hazard
Department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
Steps:
Step 1: Submit letter of request and PENRO permit/authorization to the LDRRMD office
Step 2: Agency staff receives and examines documents (5 minutes)
Step 3: Agency schedules the tree removal date
Step 4: LDRRMD staff notifies the nearest Barangay Quick Response Team of the location (5 minutes)
Step 5: LDRRMD staff performs the tree or branch removal on-site (3 hours)

subservice: Request for the Maintenance of Dangling Electrical Cables (Non-Live Wires)
department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
text:
Service: Disaster and Weather
Sub-service: Request for the Maintenance of Dangling Electrical Cables (Non-Live Wires)
Department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a letter of request to the LDRRMD office
Step 2: Agency receives and examines the request
Step 3: Agency schedules the maintenance activity
Step 4: LDRRMD staff notifies the nearest Barangay Quick Response Team
Step 5: LDRRMD staff conducts the maintenance of dangling electrical cables

subservice: Request for Food Assistance or Transportation for a Patient
department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
text:
Service: Disaster and Weather
Sub-service: Request for Food Assistance or Transportation for a Patient
Department: CITY DISASTER RISK REDUCTION AND MANAGEMENT DIVISION - MO
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request to the LDRRMD office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews the request
Step 3: Agency assesses the nature of assistance needed (food or transportation)
Step 4: Agency dispatches appropriate resources and provides the requested assistance

subservice: Issuance of Zoning Certificate
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Issuance of Zoning Certificate
Department: CITY PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit the application form with required documents to the CPDO
Step 2: Agency reviews the Zoning Application and required documents (included in processing)
Step 3: Agency provides an Order of Payment
Step 4: Agency processes the Zoning Certificate
Step 5: Client pays at the Treasury Office according to the fee schedule
Step 6: Treasury issues official receipt
Step 7: Client receives the signed Zoning Certificate from agency (26 minutes total)

subservice: Application for Preliminary Approval-Locational Clearance (PALC) and Development Permit
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Application for Preliminary Approval-Locational Clearance (PALC) and Development Permit
Department: CITY PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a notarized Application Form with all required documents to the CPDO
Step 2: Agency examines the application; if complete, stamps as received and forwards to City Council; if incomplete, returns documents
Step 3: Client obtains a copy of the City Council Resolution and Ordinance
Step 4: Client receives an Order of Payment from the agency
Step 5: Agency processes the PALC and Development Permit
Step 6: Client pays at the Treasury Office according to the fee schedule and submits photocopy of receipt
Step 7: Client obtains official receipt from Treasury
Step 8: Client receives the signed PALC and DP along with approved subdivision plans (25 minutes total)

subservice: Application for Vacant Positions
department: CITY HUMAN RESOURCE AND MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Application for Vacant Positions
Department: CITY HUMAN RESOURCE AND MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application requirements via email to the CHRMO
Step 2: Agency receives and verifies completeness of submitted documents (5 minutes)
Step 3: Agency evaluates requirements against competency-based qualification standards (10 minutes)
Step 4: Agency notifies applicant of evaluation results and invites qualified candidates for assessment (3 working days)
Step 5: Agency conducts assessment activities (45 minutes)
Step 6: Agency consolidates assessment results and transmits to HRMPSB (3 working days)
Step 7: Agency rates all applicants individually (7 days)
Step 8: Agency prepares comparative assessment results matrix (4 hours)
Step 9: HRMPSB deliberates and finalizes selection line-up (2 hours)
Step 10: HRMPSB prepares and signs resolution (1 working day)
Step 11: Selection line-up transmitted to appointing authority (5 minutes)
Step 12: Appointing authority selects qualified applicant for appointment
Step 13: Agency notifies all applicants of results (3 working days)

subservice: Complaint Regarding Environment and Pollution
department: CITY ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES DEPARTMENT
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Complaint Regarding Environment and Pollution
Department: CITY ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit complaint letter or provide information via phone call; agency receives and schedules inspection (5 minutes)
Step 2: Environmental Management Specialist II conducts actual site inspection (4 hours)
Step 3: Senior Environmental Management Specialist conducts technical meeting with the respondent/company (4 hours)
Step 4: Agency creates Compliance Monitoring and Verification Report (CMVR) within 3 days; if compliant, issues certification; if non-compliant, schedules second technical meeting
Step 5: If still non-compliant after second meeting, agency refers case to City Legal Office for Cease and Desist Order or Closure Order (4 hours)

subservice: Certificate of No Objection as requirement for Issuance of permit to cut trees
department: CITY ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES DEPARTMENT
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Certificate of No Objection as requirement for Issuance of permit to cut trees
Department: CITY ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request letter and all required documents to the CENRO
Step 2: Agency accepts and reviews submitted documents; provides inspection schedule
Step 3: Agency conducts actual field inspection of the property
Step 4: Agency evaluates the inspection report and determines approval or disapproval; communicates decision to client
Step 5: Client receives the approved Certificate of No Objection upon approval (2 days, 4 hours, 10 minutes total)

subservice: Enrollment for Regular and Freshmen Students
text:
Service: Education, Training, Seminars, and Competencies
Sub-service: Enrollment for Regular and Freshmen Students
Department: CITY COLLEGE OF CALAMBA
Steps:
Step 1: Complete the online pre-registration form
Step 2: Agency enrolls the student who completed pre-registration and prints the registration form
Step 3: Obtain the registration form from the Office of the College Registrar (25 minutes total)

subservice: Enrollment for Irregular and Transferee Students
text:
Service: Education, Training, Seminars, and Competencies
Sub-service: Enrollment for Irregular and Transferee Students
Department: CITY COLLEGE OF CALAMBA
Steps:
Step 1: Complete online pre-registration; agency submits registered student list to Office of the Director (5 minutes)
Step 2: Visit Office of the Director for evaluation and subject addition; agency assesses student and assigns study load (15 minutes)
Step 3: Obtain Registration Form; agency encodes study load and prints registration form (5 minutes)

subservice: Enrollment in Technical-Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Programs
text:
Service: Education, Training, Seminars, and Competencies
Sub-service: Enrollment in Technical-Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Programs
Department: CITY SOCIAL SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Inquire at the Manpower Development Division office (10 minutes)
Step 2: Apply and complete the registration form provided by registration staff
Step 3: Visit the Calamba Manpower Development Center Office to submit required documents
Step 4: Agency conducts document review (1 hour 15 minutes)
Step 5: Take aptitude test
Step 6: Finalize enrollment process (total: 1 hour 25 minutes, no fee)

subservice: Competency Assessment
text:
Service: Education, Training, Seminars, and Competencies
Sub-service: Competency Assessment
Department: CITY SOCIAL SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Inquire about the competency assessment and complete the Self-Assessment Guide provided by the agency (60 minutes)
Step 2: Qualified candidates pay the competency assessment fee and submit proof of payment to agency
Step 3: Complete the application form and submit for evaluation; agency issues admission slip and schedules the assessment (5 minutes)

subservice: Pre-Marriage Orientation Seminar Scheduling
text:
Service: Education, Training, Seminars, and Competencies
Sub-service: Pre-Marriage Orientation Seminar Scheduling
Department: CITY POPULATION MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Present valid ID and Certificate of No Marriage (CENOMAR, if applicable); staff verifies identity (2 minutes)
Step 2: Complete and submit CPMO Form I with staff guidance (5 minutes)
Step 3: Answer the Pre-Marriage Expectation Inventory Form (MEI) with staff assistance (5 minutes)
Step 4: Receive and sign appointment slip; agency provides reminders and confirmed seminar date (3 minutes)

subservice: Pre-Marriage Orientation Seminar / Responsible Parenthood Seminar
text:
Service: Education, Training, Seminars, and Competencies
Sub-service: Pre-Marriage Orientation Seminar / Responsible Parenthood Seminar
Department: CITY POPULATION MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Present valid ID and sign the accomplished form; agency verifies identity (2 minutes)
Step 2: Complete the pre-evaluation form with agency guidance (5 minutes)
Step 3: Attend Pre-Marriage Orientation (3 hours) or Pre-Marriage Counselling (2 hours) where module contents are discussed
Step 4: Fill out the post-evaluation form with agency support (5 minutes)

subservice: Issuance of Mayor's Clearance
department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
text:
Service: Employment
Sub-service: Issuance of Mayor's Clearance
Department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Fill out application form and submit required documents to IIPESO
Step 2: Agency receives and examines documents; issues order of payment (included in processing)
Step 3: Agency processes the Mayor's Clearance
Step 4: Client pays PHP 40.00 at Treasury Office; Treasury provides official receipt
Step 5: Agency issues Mayor's Clearance and submits to Mayor's Office for approval
Step 6: Client receives the approved Mayor's Clearance (12 minutes total)

subservice: Issuance of Referral and Endorsement Letter
department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
text:
Service: Employment
Sub-service: Issuance of Referral and Endorsement Letter
Department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Present required documents; agency provides National Skills Registry System (NSRS) form (1 minute)
Step 2: Complete the National Skills Registration Form; agency identifies suitable employment opportunities and prepares endorsement/referral letter (5 minutes)
Step 3: Client receives the completed endorsement/referral letter (2 minutes)

subservice: Registration and Granting of Accreditation to Companies and Manpower Agencies
department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
text:
Service: Employment
Sub-service: Registration and Granting of Accreditation to Companies and Manpower Agencies
Department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
FOR COMPANIES/LOCAL AGENCIES BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 1: Fill out Google Form and submit required documents
FOR COMPANIES/LOCAL AGENCIES BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 2: Agency reviews documents and enters them into Public Employment Information System (PEIS); accepts documents and issues Certificate of Accreditation (7 minutes, no fee)
FOR LOCAL AGENCIES NOT BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 1: Complete Google Form and submit required documentation
FOR LOCAL AGENCIES NOT BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 2: Agency reviews documents, enters into PEIS, and provides Order of Payment; client submits Endorsement Form
FOR LOCAL AGENCIES NOT BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 3: Agency issues Order of Payment; client pays PHP 1,200 Special Mayor's Permit fee
FOR LOCAL AGENCIES NOT BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 4: Agency provides official receipt; accepts documentation from agency
FOR LOCAL AGENCIES NOT BASED IN CALAMBA — Step 5: Agency grants Special Mayor's Permit and issues Certificate of Accreditation (13 minutes total)

subservice: Application to Conduct a Local Recruitment Activity (LRA)
department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
text:
Service: Employment
Sub-service: Application to Conduct a Local Recruitment Activity (LRA)
Department: INFORMATION, INVESTMENT PROMOTIONS AND EMPLOYMENT SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents and complete the application form to IIPESO; agency accepts documents and communicates the scheduled date for the Local Recruitment Activity
Step 2: Receive the LRA permit from the issuing office; agency grants the permit to the applicant (5 minutes total, no fee)

subservice: Certified Death Certificate
text:
Service: Health
Sub-service: Certified Death Certificate
Department: CITY HEALTH SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit the death certificate to the City Health Services Office
Step 2: Administrative Aide IV examines the document (5 minutes)
Step 3: Medical Officer III verifies and signs the certificate (5 minutes)
Step 4: Administrative Aide IV records and issues the certificate; forward to the Civil Registry Office for registration (1 minute)
Step 5: Client receives the certified death certificate and proceeds to Civil Registry Office for registration

subservice: Health Permit
text:
Service: Health
Sub-service: Health Permit
Department: CITY HEALTH SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required lab tests (Chest X-ray, Urinalysis/Fecalysis, and Hepatitis Screening) to the CHO Sanitation and Information Section; agency receives, reviews documents, and conducts interview; issues Order of Payment (5 minutes)
Step 2: Client pays PHP 50.00 at City Treasury Office; Treasury issues official receipt (2 minutes)
Step 3: Client collects the Health Permit ID from agency (5 minutes)

subservice: Dental Check-up
text:
Service: Health
Sub-service: Dental Check-up
Department: CITY HEALTH SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Register patient name; Dental Aide interviews the patient and records vital signs (10 minutes)
Step 2: Dentist II or Dentist III performs the dental examination procedure (30 minutes)
Step 3: Dentist provides medication instructions to patient
Step 4: Dental Aide dispenses prescribed medicine to patient (5 minutes)

subservice: Vaccination Services
text:
Service: Health
Sub-service: Vaccination Services
Department: CITY HEALTH SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Register and submit medical records documentation; agency receives and examines documentation (5 minutes)
Step 2: Agency measures child's weight and temperature (10 minutes)
Step 3: Interview with parent/guardian regarding health education (15 minutes)
Step 4: Actual vaccine injection performed (5 minutes)
Step 5: Client receives medication if necessary; agency distributes medicine as needed (5 minutes)

subservice: Patient Consultation Services
text:
Service: Health
Sub-service: Patient Consultation Services
Department: CITY HEALTH SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit referral from barangay midwife; Administrative Aide VI receives and verifies (3 minutes)
Step 2: Nurse IV obtains vital signs and interviews patient (10 minutes)
Step 3: Medical Officer III or IV performs actual patient examination (30 minutes)
Step 4: Medical Officer provides medication prescription (included in Step 3)
Step 5: Nutrition Officer I dispenses medication (5 minutes)

subservice: Tuberculosis Consultation Services
text:
Service: Health
Sub-service: Tuberculosis Consultation Services
Department: CITY HEALTH SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Client provides sputum sample; Nurse III conducts interview, records information, and issues X-ray request (10 minutes)
Step 2: Submit X-ray results and required documents; Medical Officer III reviews X-ray results (15 minutes)
Step 3: X-ray assessment — if positive, sputum submitted for GeneXpert; if negative, specialist consultation recommended (10 minutes)
Step 4: Medical Officer provides health information regarding TB education and HIV awareness (5 minutes)
Step 5: Medical Officer III conducts HIV screening (10 minutes)
Step 6: Client receives medication or prescription as needed (3 minutes)

subservice: Infrastructure Project Inspection
text:
Service: Housing
Sub-service: Infrastructure Project Inspection
Department: CITY PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request for infrastructure project inspection to the City Planning and Development Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews the request
Step 3: Agency schedules and conducts the infrastructure project inspection
Step 4: Agency prepares and issues an inspection report
Step 5: Client receives the official inspection findings and documentation

subservice: Land Use Reclassification
text:
Service: Housing
Sub-service: Land Use Reclassification
Department: CITY PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application and required documents to the City Planning and Development Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency conducts assessment and evaluation of the land use reclassification request
Step 4: Agency processes and prepares documentation for the reclassification
Step 5: Client pays applicable fees at City Treasury Office
Step 6: Client receives the Land Use Reclassification approval and documentation

subservice: Individual/Personal Walk-in Approach for Vaccination Services
department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Veterinary Services
Sub-service: Individual/Personal Walk-in Approach for Vaccination Services
Department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit primary information and pet health records in the log sheet at the veterinary office
Step 2: Staff reviews animal records and assesses health status for vaccination eligibility
Step 3: Administrative Assistant provides payment order for dog tag
Step 4: Client pays PHP 20.00 at the City Treasury Office
Step 5: Treasury staff issues official receipt
Step 6: Client presents receipt and waits for pet vaccination
Step 7: Veterinarian vaccinates the animal and provides dog tag/vaccination card (13 minutes total)

subservice: Provision of Consultation Services to Walk-In Clients
department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Veterinary Services
Sub-service: Provision of Consultation Services to Walk-In Clients
Department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Register pet information in log sheet (3 minutes)
Step 2: Present the pet's condition to the veterinarian
Step 3: Veterinarian examines the animal's condition and current treatment (30 minutes)
Step 4: Veterinarian provides medication and/or prescription with explanation (5 minutes)
Step 5: Client receives medication and/or prescription (38 minutes total)

subservice: Approval of Request for Mass Rabies Vaccination
department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Veterinary Services
Sub-service: Approval of Request for Mass Rabies Vaccination
Department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a letter of request to the veterinary office; agency records request in logbook (3 minutes)
Step 2: Await formal communication (phone call or email) regarding the scheduled vaccination service date and time; agency establishes vaccination date and time (30 minutes)

subservice: Application for Veterinary Health Certificate and Shipping Permit
department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Veterinary Services
Sub-service: Application for Veterinary Health Certificate and Shipping Permit
Department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Register on the list and provide all required information; agency examines the animal and necessary documents, prepares the certificates and permit (5 minutes)
Step 2: Agency provides an Order of Payment document
Step 3: Client pays at the Treasury Office according to the Shipping Permit Fees schedule; Treasury issues official receipt (2 minutes)
Step 4: Agency processes documentation and obtains required signatures (10 minutes)
Step 5: Client receives the Shipping Permit and Veterinary Health Certificate (1 minute)

subservice: Registration for Cat Spaying or Neutering
department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Veterinary Services
Sub-service: Registration for Cat Spaying or Neutering
Department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Visit the veterinary office's Facebook page to check announcements regarding the spaying/neutering schedule
Step 2: Proceed to the office and register before the scheduled spaying/neutering date
Step 3: Agency staff reviews registrants and creates the final list of animals approved for the procedure (5 minutes, no fee)

subservice: Financial Assistance for Returning to the Province
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Financial Assistance for Returning to the Province
Department: CITY SOCIAL SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to the City Social Services Department
Step 2: Staff receives and examines documents; collects information via intake sheet (20 minutes)
Step 3: Officer conducts home visit based on gathered information (1 hour)
Step 4: Officer processes documents and provides claim stub (5 minutes)
Step 5: Client retrieves financial assistance at the Treasury Office (within 20 days maximum after processing through Mayor's Office, Budget, Accounting, and Treasury)

subservice: Financial Assistance for Funeral Services
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Financial Assistance for Funeral Services
Department: CITY SOCIAL SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit all required documents to the City Social Services Department
Step 2: Staff examines documents and collects information using intake sheet (20 minutes)
Step 3: Social services staff conducts a home visit for verification (1 hour)
Step 4: Officer processes documents and issues a claim stub (5 minutes)
Step 5: Applicant collects the financial assistance from the Treasury Office (within 20 days maximum)

subservice: Kahilingan para sa Paggawa ng Certificate of Indigence
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Kahilingan para sa Paggawa ng Certificate of Indigence
Department: CITY SOCIAL SERVICES DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit all required documents to the City Social Services Department
Step 2: Staff examines submitted papers and collects information using intake sheet; claim stub issued (20 minutes)
Step 3: Social worker conducts field visit for verification (20 minutes)
Step 4: Social worker prepares the Certificate of Indigence (10 minutes)
Step 5: Staff provides the completed Certificate of Indigence to the applicant (3 minutes)

subservice: Medical/Hospitalization/Burial Assistance
department: OFFICE FOR THE SENIOR CITIZENS AFFAIRS
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Medical/Hospitalization/Burial Assistance
Department: OFFICE FOR THE SENIOR CITIZENS AFFAIRS
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application and required documents to the Office for Senior Citizens Affairs
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews submitted documents
Step 3: Agency processes the medical/hospitalization/burial assistance request
Step 4: Agency forwards documents for approval through Mayor's Office, Budget, Accounting, and Treasury
Step 5: Client receives the financial assistance (within 20 days)

subservice: Financial Aid for Individuals
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Financial Aid for Individuals
Department: OFFICE OF THE CITY VICE-MAYOR
Steps:
Step 1: Submit Letter of Request and Voter's ID/Certificate to the Office of the City Vice-Mayor; agency reviews documents (10 minutes)
Step 2: Documents forwarded to City Vice-Mayor for endorsement and approval (15 minutes)
Step 3: Agency explains procedure and provides claim stub to client (3 minutes)
Step 4: Client collects financial aid at the Office of the City Vice-Mayor (within 20 working days due to processing through Mayor's Office, Budget Office, Accounting Office, and Treasury Office)

subservice: Hospitalization and Medical Assistance
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Hospitalization and Medical Assistance
Department: OFFICE OF THE CITY MAYOR
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application through MOPAC system to receive MOPAC receipt
Step 2: MOPAC staff verifies receipt presence and requirement completeness
Step 3: Deliver documents to assigned personnel for hospitalization assistance processing
Step 4: Staff receives and validates document completeness and authenticity
Step 5: Application undergoes approval and endorsement process
Step 6: Documentation recorded and forwarded to Executive Assistant IV or City Mayor for approval
Step 7: Intake sheet preparation and requisite validation
Step 8: Application receives final approval
Step 9: Approved request and intake sheet endorsed to Finance Committee (15 minutes processing; up to 20 days for check disbursement)

subservice: Solicitation Letter
text:
Service: Social Services
Sub-service: Solicitation Letter
Department: OFFICE OF THE CITY MAYOR
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application through MOPAC system to receive MOPAC receipt; staff verifies receipt and requirements (3 minutes)
Step 2: Submit requirements including solicitation letter and voter ID photocopy; designated staff receives, examines, and stamps documents (10 minutes)
Step 3: Obtain certification of received solicitation letter; staff stamps and provides second MOPAC receipt copy
Step 4: Await notification by calling office to check status; Executive Assistant IV or City Mayor reviews approval (5 minutes)
Step 5: Receive approval or undergo client consultation if needed
Step 6: Await call confirming check retrieval or assistance availability (18 minutes processing; up to 20 additional days for Finance Committee)

subservice: Issuance of Zoning Certificate
text:
Service: Tax
Sub-service: Issuance of Zoning Certificate
Department: CITY PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit application form with required documents to CPDO
Step 2: Agency reviews the Zoning Application and required documents
Step 3: Agency provides an Order of Payment
Step 4: Agency processes the Zoning Certificate
Step 5: Client pays at the Treasury Office per fee schedule
Step 6: Treasury issues official receipt
Step 7: Client receives and signs a copy of the Zoning Certificate (26 minutes total)

subservice: Verification and Location of Property
text:
Service: Tax
Sub-service: Verification and Location of Property
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request and required documents to the City Assessment Office
Step 2: Agency receives and verifies the request
Step 3: Agency conducts research and locates the property in the tax records
Step 4: Agency provides the verification results and location details to the client

subservice: Request for Cancellation of Records (Tax Declaration)
text:
Service: Tax
Sub-service: Request for Cancellation of Records (Tax Declaration)
Department: CITY ASSESSMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request and required documents to the City Assessment Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews the request for cancellation
Step 3: Agency processes the cancellation of the specified tax declaration records
Step 4: Agency issues confirmation of cancellation to the client

subservice: MSME Training and Seminars
text:
Service: Agricultural
Sub-service: MSME Training and Seminars
Department: COOPERATIVES AND LIVELIHOOD DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request or register for MSME training/seminar at the Cooperatives and Livelihood Development Department
Step 2: Agency reviews the request and provides training schedule information
Step 3: Participant attends the scheduled training or seminar
Step 4: Participant receives certificate of completion or attendance

subservice: Assistance in Processing Requirements for CDA Registration
text:
Service: Agricultural
Sub-service: Assistance in Processing Requirements for CDA Registration
Department: COOPERATIVES AND LIVELIHOOD DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Submit request for CDA registration assistance to the Cooperatives and Livelihood Development Department
Step 2: Agency reviews the cooperative's current documents and status
Step 3: Agency guides and assists in preparing all required documents for CDA registration
Step 4: Agency assists in submitting completed documents to the Cooperative Development Authority

subservice: Assistance to MSMEs through Consultation
text:
Service: Agricultural
Sub-service: Assistance to MSMEs through Consultation
Department: COOPERATIVES AND LIVELIHOOD DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT
Steps:
Step 1: Visit or contact the Cooperatives and Livelihood Development Department to request MSME consultation
Step 2: Agency receives and processes the consultation request
Step 3: Agency assigns a consultant to meet with the MSME owner
Step 4: Consultation session conducted; agency provides business development advice and recommendations
Step 5: Client receives follow-up documentation or referral as applicable

subservice: Approval for External Consultation for the Treatment of Livestock
department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
text:
Service: Agricultural
Sub-service: Approval for External Consultation for the Treatment of Livestock
Department: VETERINARY SERVICES AND SLAUGHTERHOUSE MANAGEMENT OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit a request/letter to the Veterinary Services and Slaughterhouse Management Office
Step 2: Agency receives and reviews the request
Step 3: City Veterinarian approves the request for external livestock consultation
Step 4: Agency schedules and dispatches veterinary staff for on-site livestock examination and treatment
Step 5: Veterinary staff conducts consultation and provides treatment recommendations

subservice: Free Notarization of Prepared Legal Documents
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Free Notarization of Prepared Legal Documents
Department: CITY LEGAL SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Submit required documents to the City Legal Services Office; Administrative Aide IV receives and assesses completeness (5 minutes)
Step 2: Attorney III/IV or City Legal Officer examines documents for notarial requirements and signs them (5 minutes)
Step 3: Client receives documents and signs notary registry; Administrative Aide IV records in notary log and retains 2 copies for office records (5 minutes)

subservice: Request for Free Legal Counseling / Advice (Dulugang Bayan)
text:
Service: Registrations, Applications, Ordinances, and Others
Sub-service: Request for Free Legal Counseling / Advice (Dulugang Bayan)
Department: CITY LEGAL SERVICES OFFICE
Steps:
Step 1: Visit the City Legal Services Office in person with necessary documents (if required) — available Wednesdays and Fridays, 9:00am–12:00pm and 1:00pm–5:00pm
Step 2: Agency meets with client and refers to attorney on a first-come, first-served basis
Step 3: Attorney provides legal opinion/counseling
Step 4: Client signs logbook after consultation (18 minutes total)
